# Scope of Work Template — Commercial Health Center
**Project type:** `commercial_health_center`
**Use for:** Medical clinics, health centers, outpatient facilities, diagnostic centers, dental clinics, and other healthcare facilities subject to COFEPRIS regulation.

---

## Key Distinctions for Health Center Projects

Health center projects differ from other commercial types in the following critical ways:
1. **COFEPRIS compliance is mandatory throughout all phases:** Every design decision must account for COFEPRIS (Comisión Federal para la Protección contra Riesgos Sanitarios) regulations for healthcare facilities, including NOM standards for clinical spaces, infection control, medical waste flow, and accessibility
2. **Medical gas systems:** Oxygen, medical air, vacuum, and specialty gas systems are required for clinical areas; design and specification is included in engineering coordination
3. **Clinical workflow drives layout:** Patient flow, staff flow, clean/dirty separation, and infection control zoning are primary design constraints — aesthetics are secondary
4. **Accessibility standards:** Full compliance with NOM-001-STPS and COFEPRIS accessibility requirements for healthcare facilities (exceeds standard residential accessibility requirements)
5. **All landscape, irrigation, and solar systems are excluded by default** for health centers (clinical utility areas surrounding the building are not landscape scope)

---

## Phase Structure

### Phase 1 — Conceptual Design (Diseño Conceptual)
**Deliverables:**
- Design narrative focused on clinical workflow and patient experience (2–4 pages)
- Preliminary zone diagrams: clinical areas, waiting, staff areas, clean/dirty service corridors, reception
- Site placement / access / ambulance and emergency access concept
- 3D conceptual massing model
- Material and finish direction board (clinical durability and infection control emphasis)
- Space arrangement diagram showing all programmed clinical and support zones
- COFEPRIS facility category confirmed with client (general consultation, specialty clinic, diagnostic center, etc.)
- Infection control zoning concept (clean corridor / dirty corridor separation concept)

**Collaborators required:** None at this phase. All engineers and medical consultants join Phase 2.

**Revision assumptions:** 2 rounds of client revision included. Each additional round billed at MXN 12,000/day.

**Milestone trigger:** Phase 1 complete when client provides written approval of conceptual direction and clinical zoning concept.

---

### Phase 2 — Architectural Design (Proyecto Arquitectónico)
**Deliverables:**
- Floor plans all levels (1:100 scale) — all clinical and support zones
- Elevations all facades (1:100)
- Building sections minimum 3 (1:100) — including at least one through clinical core
- Roof plan
- Site plan with property boundaries, setbacks, access, ambulance drop-off, service entry, accessible parking
- Interior design criteria document (clinical durability standards, antimicrobial surfaces, infection control requirements by zone)
- Room-by-room clinical standards checklist (minimum room sizes, lighting levels, ventilation rates per COFEPRIS NOM requirements)
- Window and door schedule (including cleanroom-rated doors for clinical areas where required)
- Preliminary structural coordination sketch
- COFEPRIS compliance checklist — Phase 2: zoning compliance, accessible route compliance, clinical area minimum dimensions, medical waste flow confirmation
- Accessibility compliance matrix (all COFEPRIS and NOM accessibility requirements verified)

**Collaborators required:**
- Structural engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates; client contract is separate)
- Electrical engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates)
- Plumbing / hydraulic engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates)
- HVAC engineer — always required; clinical spaces require controlled ventilation and air pressure differential design (Oficio Taller coordinates)
- Medical equipment consultant (client's responsibility to provide; Oficio Taller coordinates space allocation)

**Revision assumptions:** 2 rounds of client revision included. Each additional round billed at MXN 12,000/day.

**Milestone trigger:** Phase 2 complete when client provides written approval and COFEPRIS pre-consultation (if required by facility type) is completed.

---

### Phase 3 — Engineering Coordination (Coordinación de Ingeniería)
**Deliverables:**
- Structural engineering drawings integrated with architectural set
- Electrical single-line diagram and panel schedules (including emergency power for clinical areas, UPS for critical systems)
- Lighting layout plan with fixture specifications (clinical lighting levels per COFEPRIS NOM standards; exam room, procedure room, and surgical lighting specifications)
- HVAC system design — always required: pressure differential zoning, ventilation rates per COFEPRIS, filtration specifications (HEPA where required), equipment schedules
- Plumbing and hydraulic layout (including medical-grade hand-washing stations per COFEPRIS requirements)
- Medical gas systems design: oxygen, medical air, vacuum, and specialty gases as required by clinical program — piping layout, zone valve specifications, outlet locations, alarm panel locations
- Fire suppression system design (sprinkler and/or gaseous suppression for server and equipment rooms)
- Landscape architecture: excluded by default for health centers
- Irrigation: excluded by default for health centers
- Solar systems: excluded by default unless client confirms interest before Phase 2 completion
- Foundation plan and details

**Collaborators required:**
- All engineers from Phase 2 must deliver coordinated drawings
- Medical gas systems engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates)
- Fire protection engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates)

**Revision assumptions:** 1 round of coordination revision included. Changes driven by clinical program additions after Phase 2 approval are billed as change orders.

---

### Phase 4 — Executive Plans (Planos Ejecutivos)
**Deliverables:**
- Fully coordinated construction drawing set (architectural + structural + MEP + HVAC + medical gas + fire)
- Detail sheets: wall sections, clean corridor details, clinical room millwork, laboratory casework, hand-washing station details
- Finish schedule (all surfaces, all zones — with infection control surface rating for clinical areas)
- Door and window schedule with hardware specifications (including cleanroom-rated hardware for clinical zones)
- Built-in clinical millwork drawings (nurses' stations, reception counters, lab casework, medication rooms)
- Medical equipment rough-in drawings (power, data, medical gas, drainage at each equipment location)
- Technical specifications document (clinical standards throughout)
- COFEPRIS permit-ready drawing package

**Collaborators required:** All engineers from Phase 3 must deliver coordinated final drawings.

**Revision assumptions:** 1 round of coordination revision included. Client changes after executive plan approval require a change order.

---

### Phase 5 — Contractor Bidding (Licitación)
**Deliverables:**
- Bid package preparation (executive plans + spec document sent to minimum 3 contractors — health center projects require contractors with clinical construction experience)
- Bid comparison matrix (contractor name, total, key line items, timeline, clinical construction experience references, notes)
- Recommendation with rationale (including contractor clinical project experience assessment)

**Standard requirement:** Minimum 3 bids required for health center projects. Contractors must demonstrate prior experience with COFEPRIS-regulated construction. If fewer than 3 respond, escalated to client.

**Local contractor cost validation:** Bid prices reviewed against current healthcare construction cost benchmarks (MXN per sqm for health center type and complexity). Significant deviations flagged before recommending a contractor.

---

### Phase 6 — Permitting (Trámites)
**Deliverables:**
- Building permit application package (architectural + structural + MEP)
- COFEPRIS sanitary license application package (required for all healthcare facilities)
- COFEPRIS facility authorization package (Licencia Sanitaria — specific to facility category)
- Medical gas system approval package (if applicable — submitted to COFEPRIS)
- Submission to all relevant authorities (Municipio + COFEPRIS delegación)
- Tracking and correction management until all permits and licenses issued

**Jurisdiction:** Determined by project location (Municipio de [city] + COFEPRIS delegación).

**Note:** COFEPRIS Licencia Sanitaria processing timelines vary significantly by facility category and delegación. This is the most variable timeline element for health center projects.

---

### Phase 7 — Construction Administration (Supervisión Arquitectónica)
**Status:** Optional — included only if client adds supervision scope. Strongly recommended for health center projects given clinical compliance requirements.
**Deliverables (if included):**
- Weekly site visit reports with photo documentation and COFEPRIS compliance notes
- Material, finish, and clinical equipment rough-in approval sign-offs
- RFI responses to contractor
- Construction milestone confirmation for invoice triggers
- COFEPRIS inspection preparation support (pre-inspection walk-through and documentation review)
- Punch list and corrections tracking prior to COFEPRIS final inspection

---

## Landscape, Irrigation, Solar Scope

- **Landscape architecture:** Excluded by default for health centers. Clinical utility areas surrounding the building are not landscape scope. Client responsibility if desired.
- **Irrigation:** Excluded by default for health centers.
- **Solar systems:** Excluded by default. Included only if client confirms interest before Phase 2 completion.

---

## Local Contractor Cost Validation

Included in Phase 5: bid prices reviewed against healthcare construction benchmarks (MXN per sqm for health center type) before recommendation. Minimum 3 bids required. Contractor clinical experience is a required evaluation criterion.

---

## Revision Assumptions

- Phase 1 — Conceptual Design: 2 rounds included. Each additional round: MXN 12,000/day.
- Phase 2 — Architectural Design: 2 rounds included. Each additional round: MXN 12,000/day.
- Phase 3 — Engineering Coordination: 1 round included.
- Phase 4 — Executive Plans: 1 round included. Client-initiated changes after Phase 4 approval require a change order.

---

## Typical Timeline

| Phase | Typical Duration |
|---|---|
| Phase 1 — Conceptual Design | 5–7 weeks |
| Phase 2 — Architectural Design | 10–14 weeks |
| Phase 3 — Engineering Coordination | 6–10 weeks |
| Phase 4 — Executive Plans | 6–10 weeks |
| Phase 5 — Contractor Bidding | 4–6 weeks |
| Phase 6 — Permitting (building + COFEPRIS) | 12–28 weeks (COFEPRIS Licencia Sanitaria varies significantly) |
| Phase 7 — Construction Administration | Duration of construction (health center: 18–26 months typical) |
| **Total design phases (excl. permitting + construction)** | 31–47 weeks |

Note: COFEPRIS processing is the most variable timeline element. Durations are typical estimates. Actual timeline set by Pablo in `project-schedule.json`.

---

## Payment Schedule

| Milestone | Name | Percentage | Amount (TBD) | Trigger Event |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | Contract Signing | 25% | — | Signed SOW + first payment received |
| M2 | Concept Design Approved | 15% | — | Client written approval of Phase 1 and clinical zoning concept |
| M3 | Architectural Design Approved | 20% | — | Client written approval of Phase 2 architectural drawings and COFEPRIS pre-consultation complete |
| M4 | Construction Documents Delivered | 25% | — | Executive plans package delivered to client |
| M5 | COFEPRIS Permit Obtained | 15% | — | COFEPRIS Licencia Sanitaria and building permit delivered to client |

Note: Health center payment schedule includes a Phase 2 approval milestone to reflect the depth of clinical compliance work. M5 is tied to COFEPRIS permit — the critical regulatory milestone for this project type.

Amounts are calculated by Bruno using `cost-basis.json` total_estimate × percentages above.
Payment currency: MXN unless otherwise agreed in writing.
Payment method: Bank transfer to Oficio Taller CLABE account (provided in invoice).

---

## Responsibilities Matrix

| Deliverable | Responsible Party | Reviewed by |
|---|---|---|
| Conceptual design and clinical zoning | Oficio Taller | Client + medical consultant |
| Architectural drawings | Oficio Taller | Client |
| COFEPRIS compliance checklists | Oficio Taller | COFEPRIS / Client |
| Clinical room standards compliance | Oficio Taller | Client + medical consultant |
| Structural calculations | Structural engineer | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| MEP engineering | MEP engineers | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| HVAC clinical design | HVAC engineer | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| Medical gas systems design | Medical gas engineer | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| Fire protection design | Fire protection engineer | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| Medical equipment specifications | Client / medical consultant | Oficio Taller rough-in coordinates |
| Executive plans set | Oficio Taller | Client + engineers |
| Bid package | Oficio Taller | Client selects contractor |
| Permit applications (building + COFEPRIS) | Oficio Taller | Municipal authority + COFEPRIS |
| COFEPRIS Licencia Sanitaria | Oficio Taller (prepares package) | COFEPRIS |
| Construction | Contractor (with clinical experience) | Oficio Taller (if supervision in scope) |
| Payment milestone sign-off | Client | Oficio Taller invoices |

---

## Standard Exclusions

The following are explicitly **not included** in this scope:
1. Structural, electrical, plumbing, civil, HVAC, medical gas, and fire protection engineering fees (client contracts engineers directly; Oficio Taller coordinates only)
2. Permit fees, COFEPRIS fees, municipal taxes, or government charges
3. Contractor selection or construction contracts (Oficio Taller recommends; client decides)
4. Medical equipment procurement (Oficio Taller provides rough-in coordination; client procures equipment)
5. Medical furniture and casework procurement (Oficio Taller specifies; client procures)
6. Topographic survey (required — client responsibility to provide)
7. Hydrologic or soil study (client responsibility)
8. Legal review of property title or easements
9. Construction cost guarantees
10. COFEPRIS ongoing compliance or annual renewal processes after project completion
11. Hazardous materials (asbestos, lead) assessment or abatement (client responsibility if renovation)
12. Radiation shielding design for X-ray or imaging rooms (add as specialist service if program includes diagnostic imaging)
13. Landscape architecture and irrigation (excluded by default for health centers)
14. Signage and wayfinding (excluded by default; add as service if requested)

---

## Project-Type Clauses

```json
{
  "project_type_clauses": [
    {
      "clause": "health_authority_compliance",
      "title": "COFEPRIS Regulatory Compliance",
      "text": "This project is subject to COFEPRIS (Comisión Federal para la Protección contra Riesgos Sanitarios) sanitary regulations for healthcare facilities, including the requirement for a Licencia Sanitaria before the facility may open for clinical operations. Oficio Taller will prepare all COFEPRIS permit packages and support the application process. Processing timelines set by COFEPRIS are outside Oficio Taller's control and do not constitute project delays attributable to Oficio Taller. The client is responsible for COFEPRIS Licencia Sanitaria renewals and ongoing compliance after project completion."
    },
    {
      "clause": "medical_equipment_coordination",
      "title": "Medical Equipment Rough-In Coordination",
      "text": "Oficio Taller will coordinate architectural and MEP rough-in requirements (power, data, medical gas, drainage) for all medical equipment identified in the client's equipment list. The client is responsible for providing an equipment list with technical specifications before Phase 3 begins. Changes to the equipment list after Phase 3 approval will require a change order. Oficio Taller is not responsible for equipment procurement, installation, or commissioning."
    },
    {
      "clause": "clinical_standards",
      "title": "Clinical Design Standards",
      "text": "All clinical spaces in this project are designed to comply with applicable COFEPRIS NOM standards for healthcare facilities, including minimum room dimensions, ventilation rates, lighting levels, infection control surface requirements, and accessible route standards. Design is based on the clinical program and facility category confirmed by the client at project initiation. Changes to the facility category or clinical program after Phase 2 approval constitute a change order."
    },
    {
      "clause": "optional_supervision",
      "title": "Construction Supervision — Strongly Recommended",
      "text": "Construction supervision (Phase 7) is an optional service for this project type. Given the complexity of clinical construction, COFEPRIS compliance requirements during construction, and the consequences of construction deficiencies in healthcare facilities, Oficio Taller strongly recommends including construction supervision in the scope of work. If the client elects to proceed without supervision, the client accepts responsibility for verifying that construction conforms to the approved drawings and specifications."
    },
    {
      "clause": "esignature",
      "title": "Electronic Signature",
      "text": "This document is valid when signed electronically by the client and an authorized Oficio Taller representative. Electronic signatures carry the same legal weight as physical signatures under Mexican federal law (NOM-151-SCFI)."
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## Tomás Instructions

When producing `scope-of-work.json` from this template:
1. Read `area-program.json` to determine actual program size, room count, and clinical zones
2. Read `cost-basis.json` to populate payment milestone amounts (M1–M5 × percentages above — note health center percentages differ from residential)
3. Set `project_type_clauses` from the JSON block above verbatim (all five clauses required)
4. Confirm COFEPRIS facility category from seed data or state.json; note in Phase 2 deliverables
5. Confirm whether diagnostic imaging (X-ray, MRI, CT) is in program — if yes, flag radiation shielding as a required added service and note exclusion in standard scope
6. HVAC engineering is always required for health centers — do not mark as excluded
7. Medical gas systems design is required whenever clinical areas are present — do not mark as excluded unless seed data confirms purely administrative facility
8. Landscape, irrigation, and solar are excluded by default — only include if explicitly confirmed in seed data
9. Set `revision_assumptions` per phase as specified above (health center rates: MXN 12,000/day)
10. Do NOT include supervision (Phase 7) unless state.json or seed data explicitly includes it — but always note the strong recommendation in scope
11. Set minimum bid count to 3 (not 2) in Phase 5; note contractor clinical experience requirement
